team_members.name: Sergey Nazarov
team_members.notes: Highly public figure; frequent keynote speaker at SmartCon and industry conferences. Holds significant LINK allocation. Background in philosophy and management from NYU. No formal CS degree but deep crypto-native experience since ~2011.

team_members.name: Steve Ellis
team_members.notes: Technical co-founder; lower public profile than Nazarov. Engineering background from Pivotal Labs. Leads core protocol development.

team_members.name: Ari Juels
team_members.notes: Co-authored the Chainlink 2.0 whitepaper. Highly credentialed academic cryptographer. Adds significant institutional legitimacy.

team_members.name: Adelyn Zhou
team_members.notes: Led ecosystem growth and partnership announcements. LinkedIn profile publicly visible.

team_members.name: Johann Eid
team_members.role: VP of Integrations / Head of Partnerships
team_members.notes: Leads enterprise and protocol integrations. Publicly visible at conferences.

investors.notes: Disclosed publicly as major LINK holder; purchased on open market and via OTC. Exact terms NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — private OTC deals not filed publicly.
Reported LINK holder via public portfolio disclosures and blog posts. Specific round terms NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER.
Listed as early backer in multiple public sources. Exact stake NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER.
Disclosed as early investor in public interviews. Terms NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER.
September 2017 ICO raised ~$32M. 35% of 1B LINK total supply (350M tokens) sold publicly at $0.091/LINK. Source: Chainlink ICO whitepaper and CoinGecko historical data.

35% of supply (350M LINK) reserved for team/company (SmartContract Chainlink Ltd). Vesting schedule not fully disclosed publicly. Significant tokens remain in deployer wallets tracked on Etherscan. NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER for exact vesting cliff details.

30% of supply (300M LINK) reserved for node operator incentives and ecosystem grants. Released over time per operational needs. Tracked partially via Etherscan deployer wallet: 0x98c63b7b319dfbdf3d811530f2ab9dfe4983af9d.

legal_events.description: SEC complaint against Coinbase (SEC v. Coinbase, SDNY 2023) and Binance (SEC v. Binance, DDC 2023) listed LINK as an alleged unregistered security among ~60+ tokens. Chainlink Labs itself was NOT named as a defendant. This creates regulatory cloud over LINK trading in the US.
SEC v. Coinbase filing explicitly named LINK as one of the tokens the SEC alleges is an unregistered security. Chainlink Labs not a party but token classification at issue.
SEC v. Binance filing also listed LINK among tokens alleged to be unregistered securities. Same caveat: Chainlink Labs not named as defendant.

Chainlink ICO conducted via SmartContract Chainlink Ltd (Cayman Islands entity). No SEC registration filed for the token sale. No enforcement action taken against Chainlink directly as of research date.

No SEC EDGAR filings found for SmartContract Chainlink Ltd or Chainlink Labs Inc under free EDGAR full-text search. Entity does not appear to have registered securities or filed Form D (Reg D exemption) publicly. Search conducted at EDGAR: https://efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q=%22chainlink%22&dateRange=custom&startdt=2017-01-01

founder_credibility_summary: Sergey Nazarov and Steve Ellis are fully doxxed, long-tenured crypto-native founders with verifiable track records dating to 2014; the addition of Ari Juels (Cornell/RSA Labs) as Chief Scientist substantially elevates academic and cryptographic credibility. No fraud allegations, rug-pull history, or anonymous team concerns exist, though neither founder holds a traditional computer-science or finance credential.
vc_overhang_summary: The most significant overhang is the team/company reserve of 350M LINK (~35% of supply) held by SmartContract Chainlink Ltd, whose vesting schedule is not fully public; Etherscan shows the deployer wallet still holds hundreds of millions of tokens that can be released at management's discretion. Institutional VC holders (Framework, a16z, Blockchain Capital) appear largely vested given the 2017 vintage, but exact lock-up terms are NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER from free public sources.
alignment_summary: Incentives are partially aligned but carry meaningful insider risk: the team's 35% reserve and undisclosed vesting create asymmetric information, and the SEC's classification of LINK as a potential unregistered security (in third-party exchange cases) could restrict US retail liquidity while insiders retain OTC exit options. The node operator reserve (30%) is designed to align long-term protocol growth with token demand, which is a positive structural feature. Overall: moderate alignment — better than many ICO-era projects, but the opaque team vesting and regulatory overhang are material retail risks.
